mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the labor market signals an impending economic contraction, prompting corporate earnings revisions downward and triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal shows no predictive power in recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in industrial production and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand meeting elastic supply, expanding profit margins and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy in out-of-sample testing, or INDPRO and HOUST diverge without corresponding SPX volatility.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds the inflation rate, real borrowing costs turn positive, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral when real rates are deeply positive, or the spread between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL shows no correlation with equity drawdowns.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating quarterly real GDP growth reflects expanding economic activity and rising corporate revenues, which mechanically supports higher equity valuations and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP acceleration consistently coincides with negative 60-day SPX returns, or the rolling correlation between GDP growth and equity returns drops below 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in total nonfarm payrolls acts as an early warning of weakening consumer spending, causing immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 rallies in the 5-day window following payroll misses, or the signal generates a negative information coefficient across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Rising private sector credit availability combined with stable consumer price inflation reduces real financing costs for corporations, fueling capital expenditure and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Equity returns turn negative when credit growth outpaces inflation, or the spread between RSAFS growth and CPIAUCSL shows no predictive power for forward SPX performance.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial output paired with a stable unemployment rate indicates a supply-constrained economy operating at full capacity, which historically precedes earnings upgrades and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when industrial capacity utilization peaks, or SPX volatility spikes despite stable labor and strong output.
